The '''Leveler I''' was a self propelled medium [[Concussion missile launcher/Legends|concussion missile launcher]] produced by [[Bryn & Gweith]].

==Description==
The Leveler I comprised twenty-eight [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missile]] tubes arranged in a four-by-seven array mounted on a heavy-track chassis. The tubes had several fire modes, including individual fire, seven tube salvos, or full launch of all twenty-eight. The design of the Leveler I meant that no ammunition that was not loaded into the twenty-eight tubes was carried. Instead, a [[repulsorlift ammo wagon]] was employed, and reloading of the weapon was handled manually. Designed for [[Artillery/Legends|artillery]] support, the Leveler I featured minimal shielding and no defensive armament. To fire on targets over the horizon, the Leveler I employed a [[Dedicated energy receptor/Legends|dedicated energy receptor]] that relayed target information to the crew's [[Holodisplay/Legends|holodisplay]] so the fire modes such as narrow, medium, or wide, could be programmed in.<ref name="ISB" />

==History==
The Leveler I was designed by [[Bryn & Gweith]], a subdivision of [[CoMar Combat Systems/Legends|CoMar Combat Systems]], as a fortified long-range siege weapon. It was standard issue to assault forces of the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]], although the inability of the tracked chassis to deal with certain extreme weather conditions was a cause of complaint.<ref name="ISB" />
